note: Grades, confidences, rationales and grade_impact are withheld. A reviewer works from the claim, the evidence and the documented rules only.

packets.claim: Kalaignar Super-Speciality Hospital — a 1,000-bed hospital with 15 operating theatres at Guindy, opened 15 June 2023 for ₹240 cr plus ₹146 cr of equipment.

proposed_components.text: A super-speciality hospital exists at Guindy and is operating.
limitations: Both GOs are identified but unread — the inference rests on their titles and abstracts.

proposed_components.text: It has 1,000 beds and 15 operating theatres.
limitations: No bed-strength notification, licensing record or completion certificate located.
No page reference to the source volume.

proposed_components.text: It was built for ₹240 cr plus ₹146 cr of equipment.
limitations: No budget line, tender, contract or expenditure record located for either figure.

proposed_components.text: It opened on 15 June 2023.
limitations: No inauguration order, press release or gazette notice located.

sources.title: Tamil Nadu Government 2021–26 achievements record (souvenir / minister-by-minister volumes)

relationship.supports: The existence of the hospital and every figure in the claim: bed count, theatre count, opening date and cost.
relationship.does_not_prove: Nothing independently — it is the government's own summary of its own work, with no page reference recorded so a reader cannot even locate it in the volume.

sources.title: Pay Ward — Formation of Pay wards in Kalaignar Centenary Super Speciality Hospital, Guindy
sources.issuing_authority: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu

relationship.supports: That the hospital exists and was operating as a functioning facility by March 2025 — a pay-ward order presupposes wards in use.
relationship.does_not_prove: The 1,000-bed capacity, the 15 theatres, the 15 June 2023 opening date, or the ₹240 cr cost. This order is about pay-ward formation and touches none of them.

sources.title: Establishment — Further continuance of 217 temporary posts attached to the hospital
sources.issuing_authority: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu

relationship.supports: That the hospital is staffed and continuing to operate into late 2025.
relationship.does_not_prove: Any element of the capacity or cost claim. Staffing continuance is independent of how many beds exist.

sources.title: Report of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India on State Finances for the year ended 31 March 2024 (Report No. 2 of 2025), Executive Summary
sources.issuing_authority: Comptroller and Auditor General of India / Principal Accountant General (Audit-I), Tamil Nadu
sources.quote: Despite the requirement of submitting Utilisation Certificates (UCs) against conditional grants within a stipulated time period, 111 outstanding UCs of ₹2,805.94 crore were pending as on 31 March 2024. Non-submission of UCs indicates the failure of the departmental officers to comply with the rules to ensure accountability.

relationship.supports: Nothing about this hospital. It is attached as independent evidence about the class of assurance this claim needs.
relationship.does_not_prove: It does not name this hospital, this department's spending on it, or any defect in this project. It is a state-level finding that released funds do not reliably evidence delivery.

relationship.supports: Nothing — recorded to make the absence visible.
relationship.does_not_prove: Without it, the 1,000-bed figure and the opening date rest solely on the government's own summary. No work-completion or commissioning document has been located.

missing_evidence: Work completion certificate or commissioning order
Tender and contract-award documents for the ₹240 cr construction
Expenditure record confirming the ₹240 cr / ₹146 cr split
Bed-strength notification or hospital licensing record
Occupancy or patient-throughput data proving the beds are in use
known_limitations: Both GOs are identified but unread — the inference rests on their titles and abstracts.
No bed-strength notification, licensing record or completion certificate located.
No page reference to the source volume.
No budget line, tender, contract or expenditure record located for either figure.
No inauguration order, press release or gazette notice located.

sources.quote: Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) (at current prices) grew at an average growth rate of 10.92 per cent from ₹17,43,144 crore in 2019-20 to ₹27,21,571 crore in 2023-24. There was 13.71 per cent growth in GSDP over 2022-23.

limitations: No completion certificate, bed notification or final bill located.
Three distinct figures bundled into one claim.

sources.title: Tamil Nadu Government 2021–26 achievements record (souvenir / minister-by-minister volumes)

relationship.supports: The completion, the bed count, the opening date and the cost.
relationship.does_not_prove: Any of them independently. No page reference recorded.

sources.title: Pay Ward — Formation of Pay wards in Kalaignar Centenary Super Speciality Hospital, Guindy
sources.issuing_authority: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu

relationship.supports: That the hospital was operating as a functioning facility by March 2025.
relationship.does_not_prove: The bed count, the opening date or the ₹240 cr cost. A pay-ward order is being used as existence evidence — legitimate, but it is not what the order is for.

sources.title: Report of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India on State Finances for the year ended 31 March 2024 (Report No. 2 of 2025), Executive Summary
sources.issuing_authority: Comptroller and Auditor General of India / Principal Accountant General (Audit-I), Tamil Nadu
sources.quote: Despite the requirement of submitting Utilisation Certificates (UCs) against conditional grants within a stipulated time period, 111 outstanding UCs of ₹2,805.94 crore were pending as on 31 March 2024. Non-submission of UCs indicates the failure of the departmental officers to comply with the rules to ensure accountability.

relationship.supports: Nothing about this hospital. Independent evidence on the reliability of spend-to-delivery assurance.
relationship.does_not_prove: It does not name this hospital or this project. State-level finding.

sources.title: Completion certificate and final bill — not located

relationship.supports: Nothing — recorded to make the absence visible.
relationship.does_not_prove: Neither located for a ₹240 cr capital project, so the completion claim itself is undocumented.
